tactics:
Market Regime: Not quite our spot yet and looks like the next oversold level will come at a period when leaders are still running hot. 

Macro: Inflation expectations continue to rise, breaking out on the 1Y and 2Y inflation swaps, even as oil prices stay flat. Yields can be pricing this in and may be signaling policy error as rates have been put on hold. Additionally, with the transition in Fed chair, the regime change that Warsh wants to bring in will shake things up in the market. Liquidity looks tight - currencies, Fed balance sheet, metals breaking down.

Bottom Up: Semiconductors are filling the gap so its critical that IGV/MAGS bears the responsibility since 40-45% of the market cannot go up without these sectors going up. We will see if semiconductors are the last mohicans since it is the only true leader this round. Banks seem important to track.
